wine_festivals.name: Bordeaux Fete le Vin
wine_festivals.address: Quais de Bordeaux, Quai des Chartrons, 33000 Bordeaux, France
wine_festivals.host_organization: CIVB (Conseil Interprofessionnel du Vin de Bordeaux) and Bordeaux City Council
wine_festivals.description: Europe's largest wine festival, held biennially on the Bordeaux quays. 70+ wine pavilions, 35,000 tasting tokens sold, world-class chateaux pouring current releases and select back vintages.

wine_festivals.name: Bordeaux Tasting
wine_festivals.address: Hangar 14, Quai des Chartrons, 33000 Bordeaux, France
wine_festivals.description: Annual consumer and trade grand tasting organised by the Bettane et Desseauve wine guide, held in Hangar 14 on the Chartrons quay. 250+ Bordeaux producers presenting current releases and en-primeur samples.

wine_festivals.name: Vinexpo Bordeaux
wine_festivals.address: Parc des Expositions de Bordeaux, Avenue Jean Gabriel Domergue, 33300 Bordeaux, France
wine_festivals.description: The world's leading international wine and spirits trade fair, held biennially at the Bordeaux exhibition centre. 2,400+ exhibitors from 40+ countries; Bordeaux first growths always prominently represented.

verified.address_quoted: Parc des Expositions de Bordeaux, Avenue Jean Gabriel Domergue, 33300 Bordeaux

wine_festivals.name: Bordeaux En Primeur Week
wine_festivals.address: Chateaux across the Medoc, Pessac-Leognan, Saint-Emilion and Pomerol, Bordeaux region, France
wine_festivals.host_organization: Union des Grands Crus de Bordeaux (UGCB)
wine_festivals.description: The annual en-primeur tasting week when the world's trade and press taste barrel samples of the new vintage at the chateaux before release. Prices set and allocations agreed; the financial heartbeat of Bordeaux.

wine_festivals.name: Marathon du Medoc
wine_festivals.description: The world's most celebrated wine-marathon, 42.2km through the Pauillac and Saint-Julien vineyards with 23 chateau wine stops en route. Costumed participants; oysters and Medoc wines at each station.

wine_festivals.name: La Jurade de Saint-Emilion
wine_festivals.address: Tour du Roi, Place du Marche, 33330 Saint-Emilion, France
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: Biannual: third Sunday of June (new harvest proclamation) and third Sunday of September (harvest ban lifting)
wine_festivals.description: The ancient Jurade brotherhood's medieval ceremonies held twice yearly in Saint-Emilion village: the June ban on harvesting and the September proclamation of the vintage from atop the Tour du Roi. Street processions and open tastings.

verified.address_quoted: Tour du Roi, Place du Marche, 33330 Saint-Emilion
